message:
fix(indexer): route in-flight byte budget through governor + fingerprint shard interiors

Two tightly-coupled changes to `src/indexer/mod.rs`:

1. `lexical_rebuild_pipeline_max_message_bytes_in_flight` now passes
   its computed byte budget through `responsiveness::effective_inflight_byte_limit(desired).max(1)`.

   Why: the pipeline previously returned a raw product of
   `batch_fetch_message_bytes_limit * (pipeline_channel_size + 1)` —
   on a 16-core dev box that compiles to ~2 GiB, which pegs memory
   even though the responsiveness governor was already enforcing a
   tighter ceiling for everything else. Routing through the governor
   picks up the `max_inflight_bytes` cap, the interactive-headroom
   floor, and live capacity scaling, so the producer respects
   machine-wide pressure the same way workers already do. The
   `.max(1)` at the tail preserves the "always serve at least one
   byte's worth of queue" invariant so the planner never starves.

2. `LexicalShardPlanShard` now carries a
   `conversation_id_fingerprint: String` — a blake3 hash over each
   shard's interior conversation IDs (little-endian i64 bytes), via
   a new `lexical_shard_conversation_ids_fingerprint(&[i64])` helper.

   Why: plan identity used to derive from each shard's range
   (`first_conversation_id`, `last_conversation_id`) and aggregate
   totals. That meant two plans covering the same range with
   different interior assignments — e.g. sparse sets `{1, 2, 4}` vs.
   `{1, 3, 4}`, same count / byte / message totals — would collide
   into identical `plan_id`s. The shard plan cache would then serve
   a stale plan after the set of indexable conversations changed
   interior-only.

   Fix threads a `conversation_ids: Vec<i64>` through the
   `flush_current_shard` closure (accumulated alongside the running
   counts), fingerprints it at flush time, stores the hex digest on
   the shard, and feeds that digest into the per-plan hash in
   `lexical_shard_plan_id` so the plan ID now responds to interior
   changes. A `debug_assert_eq!(*conversation_count,
   conversation_ids.len())` guards the accumulator invariant.
   `spawn_lexical_rebuild_packet_producer` also adds the fingerprint
   to its tracing span so rebuild logs are correlatable across
   runs.

3. Regression test
   `lexical_shard_plan_id_changes_when_sparse_assignments_change`
   exercises two 3-conversation plans with identical range / count /
   bytes but interior IDs `{1, 2, 4}` vs. `{1, 3, 4}`, asserts the
   shard-level fingerprints differ, and — more importantly — that
   the top-level `plan_id` now differs too. Complements the existing
   oversized-shard and ordering tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
